fact: May 22 is exactly 142 days into the year - that's almost exactly 39% of the whole year done!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby birds to be learning how to fly and crash-landing in people's backyards.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most of North America on this date, making it one of those annoyingly early bright mornings.
May 22 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season - the zodiac sign known for being chatty and having two personalities.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant orange and red.
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy around this time in the Northern Hemisphere, turning into those fluffy white puffballs you can blow apart.
The moon phase on May 22 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the Flower Moon because everything is blooming like mad.
In northern countries like Canada and Alaska, the sun might not set until after 10 PM on this date - talk about a long day!
May 22 is exactly one month away from the summer solstice, making it the perfect time for plants to have growth spurts.
Bees are typically at their busiest around this date in temperate regions, visiting up to 5,000 flowers per day to make honey.
In many places, this is when morning dew gets really heavy because warm days meet cool nights - perfect for finding spider webs covered in water droplets.
The Earth is tilted at just the right angle on May 22 that shadows at noon are getting shorter and shorter each day in the Northern Hemisphere.
